items.headline: SK Hynix quarterly profit jumps five-fold on strong AI chip demand
items.summary: STORY: :: SK HynixSouth Korea's SK Hynix on Thursday posted a record quarterly profit, and forecast AI chip demand would exceed manufacturing capacity.The Nvidia supplier reported an operating profit of just over $25 billion for January-March.That's a five-fold jump from last year, and in line with analyst estimates.Shares in the firm rose over 2% in morning trade, but later fell back into negative territory. The stock has jumped nearly 90% so far this year.SK Hynix said that client requests for chips over the next three years already exceed production capacity.The outlook signals strong momentum for the high bandwidth memory chips used in AI, likely keeping supplies constrained and prices high.SK Hynix said earlier it was accelerating capacity expansion.That includes bringing forward the opening of a chip plant in South Korea to meet surging demand.It sees limited impact from the war in the Middle East, having taken steps to secure inventory, diversify suppliers and lock in long-term energy contracts.

Sovereign computing refers to a country's capacity to develop and control its own AI infrastructure.

items.summary: Shares of fabless chip and software maker Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) jumped 4.3% in the afternoon session after the company announced an expanded collaboration with Google Cloud amid a broad rally in semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: Intel to Report Earnings. The Focus Is on What Comes Next.
items.summary: Intel stock has rallied sharply, but investors are focused on whether Intel can attract outside foundry customers and compete in AI chips.

items.headline: Why Walmart is rolling out AI to 2M employees
items.summary: The retailer plans to train all employees on agentic AI tools in pursuit of customer experience improvements, Chief People Officer Donna Morris said.

items.headline: Wall Street Breakfast Podcast: ServiceNow Tanks On Margins
items.summary: ServiceNow crashes as recent acquisitions lead to slightly lower-than-expected margins. Tesla investors factor in Elon Musk's warning of heavy capex spend. LULU appoints Nike exec as CEO.

items.headline: Apple (AAPL) Stock Trades Up, Here Is Why
items.summary: Shares of iPhone and iPad maker Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) jumped 2.3% in the afternoon session after the company announced that John Ternus, senior vice president of Hardware Engineering, would become its next chief executive officer, a move that analysts viewed positively.
